questions.prompt: What does 'hook rate' measure and what's a healthy baseline?

choices.text: 3-second video plays divided by impressions — the share of users who stopped scrolling long enough to absorb the first few seconds. Industry baseline 25-35%, strong 40-50%, below 20% means the hook is failing. Compared across creatives in the same ad set, the spread between top and bottom is usually 2-3x — confirming hook is the highest-leverage lever.

questions.prompt: Cleanly isolating the hook in an A/B test means what?

choices.text: Keep the body (5-30 seconds after the hook) identical across variants and only swap the first 2-3 seconds — different opening line, opening visual, or opening action. Run 5-7 variants in one ad set with equal exposure for 7-10 days; compare hook rate (3s plays/impressions). Testing full creatives against full creatives introduces too many variables to attribute the change to the hook.

questions.explanation: Hold body constant; swap only the first 2-3 seconds.
